Synagis RSV prophylaxis

Synagis RSV prophylaxis should be considered from     [**Month (only) **] through [**Month (only) 958**] for infants who meet any of the     following 3 criteria: (1) born at less than 32 weeks     gestation; (2) born between 32 and 35 weeks with 2 of the     following: Daycare during RSV season, a smoker in the     household, neuromuscular disease, airway abnormalities,     or school-age siblings; or (3) with chronic lung disease
Influenza immunization
immunization against influenza

.  Influenza immunization is recommended annually in the     Fall for all infants once they reach 6 months of age.     Before this age (and for the first 24 months of the     child's life) immunization against influenza is     recommended for household contacts and out of home     caregivers
